hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point jump to 41% signals real money moving in on shutdown resolution, betting Congress gets tired of the political theater before the deadline hits. At these odds, you're getting paid decent value for a base case where divided government actually functions, but watch the March budget negotiations in real time, if Republicans use it as leverage on other must-pass items, expect this to crater fast.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Clock Just Reset, And 41% Odds Look Cheap
daily_take.deck: The DHS shutdown question jumped 10 points overnight, signaling real market conviction that negotiations are breaking down before the March 31 deadline. But 41% for "yes, it ends" still prices in high drama when Congress has a track record of last-minute saves. Smart money should ask: does this reflect genuine political stalemate or just volatility chasing headlines? The asymmetry favors shorts here if you believe gridlock theater ends as usual.

sidebar.headline: Somalia strike odds hit 96% in 24 hours, which means either the intel is locked in or the market got ahead of itself by 5-10 points.
sidebar.label: US Strikes on Somalia (Feb): 96.2%

sidebar.headline: India T20 World Cup surge suggests money saw something about tournament seeding or odds movement in parallel markets, but 44.5% still looks fair for a 5-team race.
sidebar.label: India 2026 T20 Win: 44.5%

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO tanked 22 points in a day, a massive drop that screams either inside chatter on delays or panic selling that presents a contrarian opportunity.
